properties.remark: PUBLIC WITNESSED AND VIDEO RECORDED A TORNADO OVER CONAWAY RANCH PRESERVE FRIDAY EVENING. HE REPORTED OBSERVING THE TORNADO TOUCH DOWN TO THE SOUTHWEST OF HIS LOCATION. HE ESTIMATED IT TOUCHED DOWN BETWEEN COUNTY ROADS 103 AND 25 AND TRAVELED NORTH/NORTHEAST FOR ABOUT 100 YARDS. IT THEN LIFTED BACK TO A FUNNEL CLOUD FOR ABOUT 400-500 YARDS BEFORE TOUCHING BACK DOWN IN A FLOODED RICE FIELD AND TRAVELING FOR ANOTHER APPROXIMATELY 100 YARDS BEFORE LIFTING BACK INTO THE CLOUD NEAR THE LEVEE. NO VISIBLE DAMAGE OBSERVED. INTENSITY RATING: EF0 TORNADO DURATION 5 MINUTES
EF0 TORNADO HWY 99 SKYWAY RD TO PARADISE AND SOUTH TO NEAL RD 715 PM-727 PM 3/25/14 ESTIMATED 65 MPH, ROTTED TREES BLOWN DOWN TORNADO DURATION 12 MINUTES

EF1 TORNADO. TIME 530 PM TO 600 PM PDT. MULTIPLE TOUCHDOWNS ALONG COUNTY ROAD 39 BETWEEN ROADS P AND TT. DAMAGE PATH 24 FEET WIDE BY 1500 FEET LONG. WINDS UP TO 100 MPH WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO ALMOND ORCHARDS AND OUTBUILDINGS. ABOUT 60 ALMOND TREES UPROOTED.
BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN ON PASTURE LAND OF RANCH, NO DAMAGE REPORTED. ESTIMATED RATING EF0. TOUCHDOWN PHOTOGRAPHED BY PUBLIC, RELAYED BY MEDIA. TORNADO DURATION 5 MINUTES

EF1 TORNADOES. TIME 530 PM TO 600 PM PDT. TWO TORNADOES TOUCHED DOWN NORTH AND SOUTH OF CR39, BETWEEN ROADS R AND TT. NORTHERN TORNADO, DAMAGE PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 2700 FEET. OUTSHED BUILDING AND PHEASANT PEN DAMAGED NEAR ROADS T AND TT. SOUTHERN TORNADO, DAMAGE PATH LENGTH APPROXIMATELY 9100 FEET. TORE SHEET METAL OFF MULTIPLE BARNS/OUTBUILDINGS,DESTROYED A GROVE OF TREES IMPACTING OPERATIONS AT LOCAL DAIRY. TORNADO TRACKED NEAR CR 41 BETWEEN ROADS R AND TT.

EF1 TORNADO. TIME 530 PM TO 600 PM PDT. MULTIPLE TOUCHDOWNS ALONG COUNTY ROAD 39 BETWEEN ROADS P AND TT. DAMAGE PATH 24 FEET WIDE BY 1500 FEET LONG. WINDS UP TO 100 MPH WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO ALMOND ORCHARDS AND OUTBUILDINGS. ABOUT 60 ALMOND TREES UPROOTED.

ROOF DAMAGE, 2 WINDOWS BLOWN OUT, 25-30 FEET OF FENCE BLOWN DOWN, PROJECTILE DAMAGE TO STUCCO. TORNADO DURATION 5 MINUTES
